moduleDescription:
This module implements a reStructuredText (RST) and Markdown parser. User's manual on supported markup syntax and command line usage can be found in Nim-flavored Markdown and reStructuredText.
  • See also Nim DocGen Tools Guide for handling of .nim files.
  • See also packages/docutils/rstgen module to know how to generate HTML or Latex strings (for embedding them into custom documents).

Choice between Markdown and RST as well as optional additional features are turned on by passing options: RstParseOptions to proc rstParse.

entries.name: RstParseOption
entries.code:
RstParseOption = enum
  roSupportSmilies,         ## make the RST parser support smilies like ``:)``
  roSupportRawDirective,    ## support the ``raw`` directive (don't support
                             ## it for sandboxing)
  roSupportMarkdown,        ## support additional features of Markdown
  roPreferMarkdown,         ## parse as Markdown (keeping RST as "extension"
                             ## to Markdown) -- implies `roSupportMarkdown`
  roNimFile,                ## set for Nim files where default interpreted
                             ## text role should be :nim:
  roSandboxDisabled          ## this option enables certain options
                             ## (e.g. raw, include, importdoc)
                             ## which are disabled by default as they can
                             ## enable users to read arbitrary data and
                             ## perform XSS if the parser is used in a web
                             ## app.

entries.name: RstParseOptions
entries.code: RstParseOptions = set[RstParseOption]

entries.name: MsgClass
entries.code:
MsgClass = enum
  mcHint = "Hint", mcWarning = "Warning", mcError = "Error"

entries.name: MsgKind
entries.code:
MsgKind = enum
  meCannotOpenFile = "cannot open \'$1\'", meExpected = "\'$1\' expected",
  meMissingClosing = "$1",
  meGridTableNotImplemented = "grid table is not implemented",
  meMarkdownIllformedTable = "illformed delimiter row of a Markdown table",
  meIllformedTable = "Illformed table: $1",
  meNewSectionExpected = "new section expected $1",
  meGeneralParseError = "general parse error",
  meInvalidDirective = "invalid directive: \'$1\'",
  meInvalidField = "invalid field: $1",
  meFootnoteMismatch = "mismatch in number of footnotes and their refs: $1",
  mwRedefinitionOfLabel = "redefinition of label \'$1\'",
  mwUnknownSubstitution = "unknown substitution \'$1\'",
  mwAmbiguousLink = "ambiguous doc link $1",
  mwBrokenLink = "broken link \'$1\'",
  mwUnsupportedLanguage = "language \'$1\' not supported",
  mwUnsupportedField = "field \'$1\' not supported",
  mwRstStyle = "RST style: $1",
  mwUnusedImportdoc = "importdoc for \'$1\' is not used",
  meSandboxedDirective = "disabled directive: \'$1\'"

entries.name: MsgHandler
entries.code:
MsgHandler = proc (filename: string; line, col: int; msgKind: MsgKind;
                   arg: string) {.closure, gcsafe.}
entries.description: what to do in case of an error

entries.name: FindFileHandler
entries.code: FindFileHandler = proc (filename: string): string {.closure, gcsafe.}

entries.name: FindRefFileHandler
entries.code:
FindRefFileHandler = proc (targetRelPath: string): tuple[targetPath: string,
    linkRelPath: string] {.closure, gcsafe.}
entries.description: returns where .html or .idx file should be found by its relative path; linkRelPath is a prefix to be added before a link anchor from such file

entries.name: rstnodeToRefname
entries.code: proc rstnodeToRefname(n: PRstNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: addNodes
entries.code: proc addNodes(n: PRstNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: getFieldValue
entries.code:
proc getFieldValue(n: PRstNode; fieldname: string): string {.gcsafe, raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: getArgument
entries.code: proc getArgument(n: PRstNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: RstFileTable
entries.code:
RstFileTable = object
  filenameToIdx*: Table[string, FileIndex]
  idxToFilename*: seq[string]

entries.name: PRstSharedState
entries.code: PRstSharedState = ref RstSharedState

entries.name: EParseError
entries.code: EParseError = object of ValueError

entries.name: LineRstInit
entries.code: LineRstInit = 1
entries.description: Initial line number for standalone RST text

entries.name: ColRstInit
entries.code: ColRstInit = 0
entries.description: Initial column number for standalone RST text (Nim global reporting adds ColOffset=1)

entries.name: ColRstOffset
entries.code: ColRstOffset = 1
entries.description: 1: a replica of ColOffset for internal use

entries.name: whichMsgClass
entries.code: proc whichMsgClass(k: MsgKind): MsgClass {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: returns which message class k belongs to.

entries.name: defaultMsgHandler
defaultFindFile
entries.code:
proc defaultFindFile(filename: string): string {.raises: [],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect], forbids: [].}

entries.name: defaultFindRefFile
entries.code:
proc defaultFindRefFile(filename: string): (string, string) {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: addFilename
entries.code:
proc addFilename(s: PRstSharedState; file1: string): FileIndex {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns index of filename, adding it if it has not been used before

entries.name: setCurrFilename
entries.code:
proc setCurrFilename(s: PRstSharedState; file1: string) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: newRstSharedState
rstMessage
entries.description: Print warnings using info, i.e. in 2nd-pass warnings for footnotes/substitutions/references or from rstgen.nim.

entries.name: addAnchorNim
entries.description: Adds an anchor refn, which follows the rule arNim (i.e. a symbol in *.nim file)

entries.name: safeProtocol
entries.code:
proc safeProtocol(linkStr: var string): string {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: getFieldValue
entries.code: proc getFieldValue(n: PRstNode): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the value of a specific rnField node.

This proc will assert if the node is not of the expected type. The empty string will be returned as a minimum. Any value in the rst will be stripped form leading/trailing whitespace.

entries.name: rstParsePass1
entries.description: Parses an RST fragment. The result should be further processed by preparePass2 and resolveSubs (which is pass 2).

entries.name: preparePass2
entries.description: Records titles in node mainNode and orders footnotes.

entries.name: resolveSubs
entries.description: Makes pass 2 of RST parsing. Resolves substitutions and anchor aliases, groups footnotes. Takes input node n and returns the same node with recursive substitutions in n.sons to result.

entries.name: completePass2
entries.code:
proc completePass2(s: PRstSharedState) {.raises: [ValueError, Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

entries.name: rstParse

signature.return: tuple[node: PRstNode, filenames: RstFileTable, hasToc: bool]

entries.description: Parses the whole text. The result is ready for rstgen.renderRstToOut, note that 2nd tuple element should be fed to initRstGenerator argument filenames (it is being filled here at least with filename and possibly with other files from RST .. include:: statement).
